dialogues.text: I need thirty. I'd rather not get into the why of it standing out here. You know I don't ask unless it's real.
dialogues.yes_response: I'll pay it back. You know I'm good for it. I've always been good for it, whatever else I haven't been good for.
dialogues.no_response: Fair enough. I'd have said the same at your age. Probably did, to my old man.

dialogues.text: Truck needs work. The kind I used to do myself, in the driveway, before my hands stopped agreeing with me. Thirty gets it looked at.
dialogues.yes_response: I'll get it sorted this week. Been meaning to. I've been meaning to do a lot of things this week.
dialogues.no_response: I'll manage. Always have. The truck and me, we understand each other's bad habits.

dialogues.text: I've been thinking I should call you more. Thought it three or four times this month and didn't do it once. Can you spare thirty.
dialogues.yes_response: I'm going to call more. I mean that. Starting this week. Don't hold your breath, but — I mean it.
dialogues.no_response: Alright. That's alright. I had it coming, the no. I know how the math on that works.

dialogues.text: Your sister's been asking after you. She worries — does it quietly, like your mother. Don't tell her I told you. Thirty.
dialogues.yes_response: She'd want to know you're alright. She just never learned the words for asking. Gets that from me.
dialogues.no_response: She'll worry regardless. That's the engine she runs on. Always has been.

dialogues.text: I want to take your mother somewhere with a tablecloth. Somewhere she has to think about what to wear. Haven't done that in years. Thirty would start it.
dialogues.yes_response: She'd like that. She doesn't ask for much. Never has, which is the part I keep failing to deserve.
dialogues.no_response: Maybe another time. It'll keep. We're good at keeping, your mother and me.

dialogues.text: I'm not good at saying things. Never have been, you know that better than anyone. So I'll say the easy thing. Thirty.
dialogues.yes_response: I'm going to do better. I mean it. I should've been better while it counted for more.
dialogues.no_response: Alright. I understand. You don't owe me the yes. I'm aware of the books between us.

dialogues.text: Got a call from the hospital. About the billing end of things, not the rest. I covered it so your mother wouldn't see it first. I need thirty back.
dialogues.yes_response: Don't you worry about that part. You focus on getting through the day. That's your whole job today.
dialogues.no_response: I'll sort it. Don't worry about it. That's a thing I can still do for this family. Let me.

dialogues.text: I'm not good at this. Any of it — the talking, the showing up, all of it. I want you to know I tried, in my way. Thirty.
dialogues.yes_response: You didn't get that from me. Being good at things, the soft kind. That's all you. All your own doing.
dialogues.no_response: Alright. I know. I'm not going to argue the verdict. I helped write it.

dialogues.text: I should've come to more of your things. The games, the school nights, all of it. I know that. I've always known it. Thirty.
dialogues.yes_response: I'll make it up to you somehow. I don't know how yet. But I've got it in mind now, if that counts.
dialogues.no_response: Yeah. I hear you. That's the right answer, probably. Doesn't make it land easy.

dialogues.text: I know I haven't been — I don't have the end of that sentence. Never have. Thirty, and we'll let the sentence go unfinished like the rest of them.
dialogues.yes_response: You're a good kid. You always have been. I should've said it more, while it could've done you some good.
dialogues.no_response: Alright. Take care of yourself out there. That part I do know how to say.
